# nayatel_ssh_key (Resource)

Manages an SSH key in Nayatel Cloud.

-> Use the `fingerprint` attribute to reference the key from `nayatel_instance.ssh_fingerprint`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "nayatel_ssh_key" "main" {
  name       = "deploy-key"
  public_key = "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3... user@host"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the SSH key. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `public_key` (String) The public key content (e.g., 'ssh-ed25519 AAAA...' or 'ssh-rsa AAAA...'). Changing this forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `fingerprint` (String) The fingerprint of the SSH key (returned by the API)
- `id` (String) SSH key identifier (same as name)
## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# SSH keys are imported by key name
terraform import nayatel_ssh_key.main deploy-key
```
